description: AI output optimization (chart/table tabs, methodology popovers, suppress formula text) and custom dashboards (create, build via sidebar chat, save, refresh)

userStories.title: Extend chart specs with table data and methodology metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need chart spec types to carry methodology info so the UI can render formulas on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat ChartSpec in src/lib/charting/spec.ts gains optional methodology?: { formula?: string; description?: string; assumptions?: string[] }
Dashboard ChartSpec in src/lib/dashboard/types.ts gains same optional methodology field
parseChartSpec in types.ts parses methodology from raw JSON if present (formula: string, description: string, assumptions: string[])
normalizeChartSpec in spec.ts passes through methodology field if present in raw input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Two separate ChartSpec types exist: one for chat (src/lib/charting/spec.ts) and one for dashboard (src/lib/dashboard/types.ts). Both need the methodology field. The chat spec has normalizeChartSpec(); the dashboard spec has parseChartSpec(). Keep changes minimal — just add the optional field and parse it.

userStories.title: Chart/Table tab toggle for chat chart blocks
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between Chart and Table views on every chart in chat messages.
acceptanceCriteria: ChartBlock component (src/components/chat/chart-block.tsx) wraps content in shadcn Tabs with two tabs: Chart (default) and Table
Chart tab renders existing ChartFromSpec unchanged
Table tab renders chart data array as shadcn Table: headers from xAxisKey + metrics keys, rows from data, numeric values locale-formatted, scrollable container
Tab switching is instant — both views pre-rendered (no lazy load)
Full-screen dialog also includes the Chart/Table tabs
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Use existing Tabs component from src/components/ui/tabs.tsx and Table from src/components/ui/table.tsx. The chart data is in result.spec.data (array of objects). For column headers, use [spec.xAxisKey, ...spec.metrics.map(m => m.key)] for non-pie charts, or [spec.nameKey/xAxisKey, spec.valueKey/metrics[0].key] for pie. Format numbers with Intl.NumberFormat.

userStories.title: Update chat system prompt to suppress formula text and embed methodology
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI to stop dumping formulas as plain text and embed them in chart metadata instead.
acceptanceCriteria: CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT updated with rule: Do NOT write formulas, equations, or step-by-step calculation breakdowns as plain text in the message body
New rule: Embed analytical methodology in each chart block's methodology field: { formula?: string, description?: string, assumptions?: string[] }
New rule: Message text should contain only brief insight summary, key findings, and actionable recommendations (3-5 sentences max before/after charts)
Chart block format spec in prompt updated to show optional methodology field in the JSON schema
Existing tool workflow section preserved unchanged
Existing output format rules (chart/table/kpi block syntax) preserved unchanged
Existing source and schema integrity rules preserved unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify src/app/api/chat/_lib/system-prompt.ts. Add the methodology suppression rules to the 'Output format rules' section. Add methodology to the chart JSON example. Keep all existing sections intact — this is additive. The key behavioral change: AI should produce cleaner, more concise text with rich chart metadata.

userStories.title: Methodology popover on chart blocks
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click an info button on a chart to see the formula/methodology used.
acceptanceCriteria: When methodology exists on a chart spec, ChartBlock renders a small info icon button (lucide Info or Calculator icon) next to the existing Full screen button
Clicking the button opens a shadcn Popover showing: formula in monospace/code font, description as paragraph, assumptions as bulleted list
If no methodology data exists on the spec, the info button is not rendered at all
Popover styled with dark theme: border-border, bg-card, text-foreground
Works in both inline and full-screen dialog views
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/chat/chart-block.tsx. Use Popover from shadcn (may need to add via shadcn CLI if not present: check src/components/ui/popover.tsx). The methodology field is on result.spec.methodology after US-001. Place the button in the same flex row as the Full screen button.

userStories.title: Chart/Table tabs on dashboard charts
userStories.description: As a user, I want the same Chart/Table toggle on AI-generated dashboard charts.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardClient (src/components/dashboard/dashboard-client.tsx) chart cards gain shadcn Tabs in the card: Chart (default) | Table
Table view renders chart data array as formatted Table (same approach as US-002: headers from keys, formatted values, scrollable)
KPI cards are NOT affected — no table view for KPIs
Chart grouping sections still render correctly with tabs present
Methodology info button also appears on dashboard chart cards when methodology field exists in ChartSpec
Extract shared ChartDataTable component to src/components/charts/chart-data-table.tsx for reuse between chat and dashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: This story should extract the table rendering from US-002 into a shared component. Then both ChartBlock (chat) and DashboardClient (dashboard) import it. The dashboard ChartSpec is from src/lib/dashboard/types.ts which has different shape (xKey/yKeys vs xAxisKey/metrics) — the shared component needs to handle both shapes or normalize to a common format.

userStories.title: Database schema for custom dashboards and widgets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DB tables to store user-created dashboards and their widget configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: New customDashboards table in schema.ts: id (uuid PK defaultRandom), userId (uuid FK→users notNull), projectId (uuid FK→projects notNull), name (varchar 255 notNull), description (text nullable), status (varchar 50 default 'active' notNull), createdAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull), updatedAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull)

New dashboardWidgets table in schema.ts: id (uuid PK defaultRandom), dashboardId (uuid FK→customDashboards notNull), widgetType (varchar 50 notNull), title (varchar 255 notNull), config (jsonb notNull), sortOrder (integer default 0 notNull), createdAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull), updatedAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull)
Both tables exported from schema.ts
Run npx drizzle-kit push successfully against Neon
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing schema patterns (uuid PKs, FK references, timestamps). Place new tables after the uploadedDatasets section. widgetType values: 'chart' or 'kpi'. config stores the full ChartSpec or KPIItem JSON.

userStories.title: Custom dashboards CRUD API
userStories.description: As a user, I need API endpoints to create, list, get, and delete custom dashboards.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/custom-dashboards — creates dashboard, body { projectId, name, description? }, validates project belongs to user and is active, returns { id, name, description, projectId, status, createdAt }
GET /api/custom-dashboards?projectId=xxx — returns user's active dashboards, optional projectId filter, each includes widgetCount, ordered by updatedAt desc
GET /api/custom-dashboards/[id] — returns dashboard with widgets array (ordered by sortOrder asc), validates ownership, 404 if not found or archived
DELETE /api/custom-dashboards/[id] — sets status to 'archived', returns { success: true }
All endpoints require auth (401 for unauth, 404 for not found, 400 for bad input)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create: src/app/api/custom-dashboards/route.ts (GET+POST), src/app/api/custom-dashboards/[id]/route.ts (GET+DELETE). Follow auth pattern: auth() → resolveAppUserId(). widgetCount via subquery or separate count query.

userStories.title: Dashboard widgets CRUD API
userStories.description: As a user, I need API endpoints to add, update, and remove widgets from a custom dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets — adds widget, body { widgetType, title, config, sortOrder? }, validates dashboard ownership, returns widget object with id
PATCH /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets/[widgetId] — updates widget title/config/sortOrder, validates ownership chain (widget→dashboard→user)
DELETE /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets/[widgetId] — deletes widget row, validates ownership
POST /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets/reorder — body { widgetIds: string[] }, updates sortOrder for each widget to match array index, validates all widgetIds belong to dashboard
All endpoints: 401 unauth, 404 not found, 400 bad input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create: src/app/api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets/route.ts (GET implicit via parent, POST+reorder), src/app/api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets/[widgetId]/route.ts (PATCH+DELETE). Config is opaque JSONB — store whatever the AI produces.

userStories.title: Custom dashboards list UI under project detail
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and create custom dashboards from the project detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: /projects/[id] page gains a Custom Dashboards section below the AI-generated dashboard content
Section header: 'Custom Dashboards' with a 'Create Dashboard' button
Fetches dashboards from GET /api/custom-dashboards?projectId=[id] on mount
Displays dashboard cards showing: name, widget count, formatted updated date
Create Dashboard button opens shadcn Dialog with name (required) and description (optional) fields
Submitting dialog calls POST /api/custom-dashboards with projectId and refreshes list
Each dashboard card is a Link to /projects/[id]/dashboards/[dashboardId]
Empty state message when no custom dashboards exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: The current /projects/[id]/page.tsx renders an AI-generated project-controls dashboard. Add the custom dashboards section below it as a separate client component. Fetch dashboards client-side via useEffect + fetch.

userStories.title: Custom dashboard canvas page with widget rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view a custom dashboard that renders its saved widgets in a grid.
acceptanceCriteria: New page: src/app/(dashboard)/projects/[id]/dashboards/[dashboardId]/page.tsx
Server component fetches dashboard + widgets via GET /api/custom-dashboards/[dashboardId]
Renders header: dashboard name, breadcrumb (Projects → Project Name → Dashboard Name), action buttons
KPI widgets (widgetType='kpi') render as styled cards (same DynamicKPICard style)
Chart widgets (widgetType='chart') render as cards with DynamicChart + Chart/Table tabs
Widgets ordered by sortOrder in responsive grid: 2-col desktop, 1-col mobile
Empty state: 'Start building your dashboard using the AI assistant' with button to open chat panel
Delete widget button on each widget card (calls DELETE endpoint, removes from grid)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: The dashboard canvas is a client component that receives server-fetched initial data. Widget rendering reuses DynamicChart from src/components/charts/dynamic-chart.tsx for chart type and the KPI card pattern from dashboard-client.tsx. The chart/table tabs pattern from US-005 should be reusable here.

userStories.title: Dashboard sidebar AI chat panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slide-out AI chat panel on the custom dashboard page to create widgets by conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom dashboard page has an 'AI Assistant' toggle button in the header area
Clicking opens a shadcn Sheet (side='right', width ~400px) with: chat message list, composer input
Chat uses useChat from ai/react connected to POST /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/chat
Chat endpoint: requires auth, validates dashboard ownership, uses streamText with Mistral
Chat system prompt is DASHBOARD_BUILDER_PROMPT (new file: src/app/api/custom-dashboards/_lib/builder-prompt.ts)
Builder prompt instructs AI to generate one chart or KPI at a time using ```chart / ```kpi format, use the dashboard project's datasets
Data tools (listDatasets, getDatasetSchema, queryDatasetRows, aggregateColumn, searchDatasets) scoped to the dashboard's projectId
Messages rendered with MessageRenderer (reuses existing component from src/components/chat/message-renderer.tsx)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Create: src/app/api/custom-dashboards/[id]/chat/route.ts for the streaming chat endpoint. Reuse createDataTools from src/app/api/chat/_lib/data-tools.ts with the dashboard's projectId. The Sheet component is at src/components/ui/sheet.tsx. The builder prompt should be simpler than the main chat prompt — focus on generating individual widgets.

userStories.title: Add-to-Dashboard button on chat-generated widgets
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click 'Add to Dashboard' on AI-generated charts/KPIs in the sidebar chat to pin them to my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart blocks and KPI blocks in the dashboard sidebar chat show an 'Add to Dashboard' button (lucide Plus icon + text)
Clicking the button calls POST /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/widgets with: widgetType ('chart' or 'kpi'), title (from chart title or kpi label), config (full parsed spec JSON)
After successful add, widget appears in the main dashboard grid (refetch or optimistic insert)
Button shows loading spinner during API call, success checkmark briefly after
Button disabled after widget is added (prevents duplicate adds) — shows 'Added ✓'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: This requires a modified MessageRenderer (or wrapper) for the dashboard chat that adds the 'Add to Dashboard' button on chart/kpi blocks. Use a callback prop pattern: the dashboard page passes an onAddWidget callback to the chat panel, which passes it to the renderer. The renderer calls it with the parsed spec when user clicks Add.

userStories.title: Dashboard refresh — regenerate widgets with latest data
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click Refresh to update all dashboard widgets with the latest uploaded data.
acceptanceCriteria: Refresh button in dashboard header triggers POST /api/custom-dashboards/[id]/refresh
Refresh endpoint: fetches dashboard's project datasets, for each widget calls LLM with widget title as context + dataset data, updates widget config in DB

LLM call uses a refresh-specific prompt: 'Regenerate this chart/KPI with the latest data. Original title: [title]. Original type: [widgetType]. Maintain the same analytical focus but use current data.'
Uses shared fetchUserDatasets and callDashboardLLM from src/lib/dashboard/generate.ts
Frontend shows loading skeleton per-widget during refresh (individual widget loading states)
Failed widget refreshes show error state on that widget but don't block others
Widget sortOrder and titles preserved after refresh
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Create src/app/api/custom-dashboards/[id]/refresh/route.ts. Process widgets sequentially or in parallel (Promise.allSettled). Each widget gets a mini-prompt: system prompt for single chart generation + user prompt with title and dataset context. Parse LLM response and update config JSONB. Return { refreshed: number, failed: number }.

userStories.title: Navigation integration and My Dashboards page
userStories.description: As a user, I want custom dashboards accessible from the sidebar and a dedicated listing page.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar nav gains 'My Dashboards' link (lucide LayoutGrid icon) between Projects and Financials
New page /dashboards/page.tsx lists all user's custom dashboards across all projects
Each card shows: dashboard name, project name (fetched/joined), widget count, last updated date
Cards link to /projects/[projectId]/dashboards/[dashboardId]
Empty state with 'Create your first dashboard from a Project page' message
Breadcrumbs on dashboard canvas page: Projects → [Project Name] → [Dashboard Name]
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx to add the nav item. Create src/app/(dashboard)/dashboards/page.tsx. The list endpoint GET /api/custom-dashboards (no projectId filter) returns all dashboards — need to join project name. Add Breadcrumb component if not present, or use simple link chain.
